behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real speech-model diarization quality (speaker labeling), which stubs can mask. Integration-only tests can verify wiring, but not whether the real model preserves labels well enough for attribution.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the actual speech-to-text API and assert transcript speaker labels remain stable and usable for attendee attribution in downstream summary input/output checks.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser/mapping logic over transcript text into a local structure. No real infra or model-quality signal is required beyond unit scope.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixture transcript snippets and assert the parser returns expected assignee/task pairs in the summary input schema object.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: This is model-output completeness/coverage. A mocked summarizer would hide omission failures; real LLM behavior must be checked.
behaviors.test_description: Feed transcripts with known per-attendee assignments to the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in live summary output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text is about routing already-identified decisions into the correct schema field (module handoff/state mapping). It does not require validating model judgment quality itself.
behaviors.test_description: Wire extractor output into the summary-structuring module in-process and verify identified decision objects land in `decisions` and are excluded from narrative-only fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher levels rejected because behavior explicitly scopes to routing of identified decisions, not real model decision identification quality or external contract behavior.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This validates whether the real summarization model preserves and places important decisions correctly. Deterministic stubs cannot reveal model placement/omission failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcripts containing explicit decision statements through the live summarizer and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and not only in narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on multiple model-dependent steps in sequence (segment transcription/summarization plus merge coherence) with cross-step state consistency.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, verify segment-by-segment processing order, then validate merged final summary coherence and coverage across segment boundaries using the real model pipeline.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end multi-step agentic orchestration with real external boundaries and round-trip delivery. Only workflow-level testing avoids false confidence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production-like path on a real meeting file: upload, real transcription API, real LLM summarization, and read back delivered structured output to confirm autonomous completion and expected schema.
